calibration_batch_summary.tone_assessment: Cooperative baseline. Zero RF-06 primary signals. Financial arrangement recently established post-divorce (filed July 2021). 36 triage keyword hits (86% received) — Melissa is dominant financial coercion direction. Phase A dominant (29/36 hits), indicating financial dynamics active throughout Phase A. Hot periods: 2024-08, 2024-06, 2023-09. CP2 critical for establishing financial arrangement context before signal classification can proceed.

calibration_questions.topic: Child support arrangement — structure and weaponization
calibration_questions.question: (a) What is the current child support arrangement — who pays whom, how much, per what order? (b) Has the child support amount or arrangement ever been used as leverage — either threatened (e.g., 'I'll take you back to court for more support') or withheld as punishment? (c) Has she ever tied co-parenting cooperation to financial behavior — e.g., 'I'll share the school schedule once you pay X' or 'I won't agree to a schedule change unless the support amount changes'?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Child support leverage is the most common RF-06 pattern. Understanding the financial arrangement structure is essential before any financial coercion signals can be correctly classified.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — establishes the financial baseline; any conditionality linking access/information to financial compliance is RF-06 primary

calibration_questions.question: (a) Are there court-ordered shared expenses (medical, activities, childcare)? (b) Have there been disputes about expense allocation — e.g., Melissa refusing to share medical bills, demanding accounting for activity costs, or refusing agreed expenses as a tactic? (c) Has she ever used Olivia's needs or wants as financial leverage — e.g., 'Olivia needs X, and since you won't pay for it, she can't have it on your time'?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Expense disputes are a secondary RF-06 mechanism. Distinguishing legitimate cost disputes from weaponized financial friction requires knowing what the actual court-ordered expense allocation is.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — confirms RF-06 Category (withholding expenses, frivolous accounting demands, children's needs as leverage)

calibration_questions.question: The triage identifies Aug 2024, Jun 2024, and Sep 2023 as RF-06 hot periods. (a) August 2024 was the EP-0854 mega-cluster (311 messages). Was financial coercion part of that conflict? (b) June 2024 overlaps with the RF-15 incest-framing cluster (B-016, Jun 30 2024) — were there financial dimensions to that conflict? (c) September 2023 — do you recall any specific financial dispute or leverage event around that time?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Correlating financial hot periods with known conflict clusters clarifies whether financial coercion is a standalone mechanism or a co-trigger with emotional/threat escalations.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: Medium-high — confirms severity context and compound patterns (RF-06+RF-04, RF-06+RF-05)

calibration_questions.question: The triage shows 5 RF-06 hits in Phase C (Oct 2025 - Apr 2026). (a) The November 2025 'big contempt case' threat (COMP-RF04-028) — were there financial dimensions, e.g., threat of financial sanctions? (b) The police report (Nov 22 2025) and subsequent visit refusals — was there any financial leverage in that period (e.g., threatening support changes as retaliation)? (c) March/April 2026 — the alcoholism accusation and bruise welfare check periods — any financial coercion in those exchanges?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Phase C financial patterns determine whether RF-06 remains active at dataset close or resolved. The Nov 2025 contempt threat and visit refusal cascade likely have financial dimensions.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — determines arc status at dataset close (ACTIVE vs. resolved)

addenda_proposed.title: Financial Arrangement Context
addenda_proposed.rule_draft: [PENDING CP2 Q1] Establish: (1) child support payer/payee/amount; (2) court-ordered expense sharing requirements; (3) any court-ordered financial provisions relevant to custody. Apply as calibration context for all financial language in the dataset. Without this baseline, financial disputes cannot be distinguished from weaponization.

addenda_proposed.title: Access-for-Money Compound Rule
addenda_proposed.rule_draft: [PENDING CP2] When financial compliance is explicitly conditioned on co-parenting cooperation (or vice versa): RF-06 primary + RF-02 compound. This is the highest-severity RF-06 pattern — children's access used as financial leverage.

post_cp2_instructions.if_no_reclassification: Confirm addenda. Initialize ARC-RF06-001 at CANDIDATE. Resume B-002. Cross-reference RF-05 for financial threat compounds (RF-06+RF-05 when financial leverage is delivered via threat mechanism). Cross-reference RF-02 for access-conditional-on-financial-compliance compounds. Phase A heavy signal (29 hits) — expect steady signal from B-006 onward.
post_cp2_instructions.signal_sourcing_note: 86% received direction — Melissa is overwhelmingly the financial coercion source. Eli-sent financial signals may represent legitimate cost-sharing requests or responses to financial pressure. Distinguish clearly. August 2024 mega-cluster and June 2024 incest-framing period are highest-priority signal batches.
